category_title: Fourth-grade students with behavioral challenges and poor work completion despite adequate literacy skills
student_description: This student has adequate cognitive and literacy skills but struggles with work completion and motivation, often resisting pull-out interventions because they resent missing homeroom homework time. They exhibit a positive illusory bias, believing they are already highly competent, which requires shifting focus to behavioral goals, tangible tracking, and school-wide reinforcement to build self-management without triggering defensiveness.

criteria.rationale: Concrete tracking of personal goals builds self-management skills and provides objective proof of progress, countering the student's limited self-determined behaviors.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes a system for the student to write down personal goals, track them tangibly (e.g., with checkmarks on goal cards), and celebrate when they are met.

criteria.rationale: Tying intervention rewards to the broader school system makes the student feel special and connects the isolated pull-out group to the broader school ecosystem.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates the school-wide reinforcement system (e.g., a ticket system) alongside behavior-specific praise.

criteria.rationale: The student deeply resents missing homeroom homework time, so the intervention schedule must avoid creating an unequal homework burden compared to peers.
criteria.check: The long-term plan explicitly avoids scheduling the intervention block during the student's homeroom homework or preferred classwork time.
